relations.note: Declared background color enables the assembler to enforce no adjacent dark sections.
Declared consumed footnote symbols support assembly-time assignment.
Self-contained blocks reinforce that adjacent sections must not depend on locked components.

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.intent: Ensure email-client rendering compatibility across HEX/px/inline-CSS conventions.
rules.rule_text: Code conventions: all colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.

rules.intent: Enable direct-stack modular assembly with predictable section geometry.
rules.rule_text: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px (size.email.body_width_600 = 600px) with its own top/bottom 32px padding (padding.section.vertical_32 = 32px) and a declared background color; there must be no margins outside the section — assembly is direct stacking of blocks.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Provide the assembler the metadata needed to sequence sections and manage footnotes/CTAs.
rules.rule_text: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, which footnote symbols (†, ‡) it consumes (assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

quotes: Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Prevent footnote-symbol collisions across independently authored sections.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols are superscript and assigned in order of appearance at assembly time. When more than one referenced claim exists, sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

rules.intent: Keep author sections self-contained so locked components can be swapped without breaking visuals.
rules.rule_text: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave meant to continue into the footer).

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Preserve visual rhythm and alternation between sections during assembly.
rules.rule_text: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).
